rag_rules: Insert annex content only into the intended owner scope and preserve all existing AADL structure outside the new annex block.
Keep references grounded in identifiers that are valid and visible in the chosen owner scope; do not introduce references to unrelated model properties or hidden subcomponent ports.
Use implementation scope only for implementation-level content that depends on subcomponents or connections; otherwise preserve component-level contract ownership decisions from upstream artifacts.
generic_rules: Preserve component types, implementations, features, connections, properties, and ordering unless the insertion itself requires minimal structural change.
Do not move, rename, or rewrite existing architectural elements when adding an annex block.
Maintain scope discipline: content referring to external features belongs with the owning component scope, while internal binding logic stays with implementation scope when valid.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is wrapped in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-level AGREE content should appear in a valid implementation scope, typically after relevant subcomponents and connections when such dependencies exist.
anti_patterns: Do not place references to subcomponent ports in a higher-level scope where they are not directly visible.
Do not treat AADL property names or unit tokens as in-scope AGREE variables.
Do not make broad architectural edits or choose a different owner than the one designated by upstream analysis.
irrelevant_topics: Requirement semantics about when an output should hold.
Clause generation details such as assume/guarantee selection or expression design.
Validator-driven repair actions, since no diagnostics are active.
